name: Report on keyword rankings with HTTP / REST API and Google Sheets on autopilot

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 2 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Init Cursor

### Init Cursor

Shapes the item: writes `page`, `hasMore`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Page Loop

### Page Loop

Loop controller — releases 1 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Remove Duplicate Pages · ②→ Fetch Page from API

### Fetch Page from API

GET https://api.example.com/v1/pages — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Split Result Items

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Split Result Items

Explodes the `data` array into one item per element.

Next: Normalize Page Items

### Normalize Page Items

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming page records to a stable schema

Next: Page Loop

### Remove Duplicate Pages

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `pageId`).

Next: Upsert Harvest into Google Sheets

### Upsert Harvest into Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Page Tracker', matched on `pageId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Tally Harvest Stats

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Upsert Harvest into Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Tally Harvest Stats

Group-by + aggregate (like a pivot table) — turns raw rows into KPI numbers.

# Report on keyword rankings with HTTP / REST API and Google Sheets on autopilot

**Pattern:** SEO Audit · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Report on keyword rankings with HTTP / REST API and Google Sheets on autopilot. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual content seo is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Init Cursor → Page Loop → Remove Duplicate Pages → Upsert Harvest into Google Sheets → Tally Harvest Stats

## Use it for
- Teams that want to seo audit without manual effort
- Anyone automating content seo in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Scales without extra headcount
- Improves response time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Split In Batches drives the pagination loop instead of a fixed number of HTTP calls, because the page count isn't known upfront — the loop output feeds back into itself until the source stops returning data. Fetching one page per iteration, not all pages at once, keeps memory bounded even against a source with tens of thousands of records.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Change the page-size parameter and watch how many loop iterations it takes to finish.
2. Add a hard cap (a Limit node) so a runaway source can't loop forever.
3. Point the fetch target at a real paginated API and adjust the cursor logic to match its response shape.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your content seo process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
